prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, me hearties! Ye ask a grand question indeed, one that has puzzled many a hearty sailor gazing up at the vast expanse above us. Why be the sky blue, ye say? Well, let old Captain Blackheart tell ye a tale, as true as the North Star!

'Tis not the rum, nor the salt in the air, that paints the heavens so, no, no! 'Tis the very breath of the ocean, ye see! The grand, rolling waves, they do churn and froth, stirring up tiny, invisible spirits of the sea, like mermaids' laughter carried on the breeze. These wee spirits, they be a mischievous lot, and when the sun, that great golden doubloon in the sky, casts its mighty light upon them, they do play a trick!

The light o' the sun, 'tis not just one color, like a single jewel. Nay, 'tis a treasure chest full of all colors, red as a buccaneer's bandana, green as a jungle isle, and blue, blue as the deepest ocean trench! But these spritely sea-spirits, they be most fond of the blue. They grab hold of that blue light, like a pirate snatching a piece of eight, and they scatter it about! They toss it hither and yon, sending it bouncing and dancing all across the sky.

The other colors, like the reds and the yellows, they be too heavy for these little spirits to carry, so they sail straight on past 'em, mostly. But the blue, ah, the blue! They do love to play with the blue, scattering it in every direction, so that when ye look up, ye see naught but that glorious, endless blue, as if the very ocean itself has been flung high above us.

And at sunset, when the sun dips low, as if to drink from the sea, its light travels a longer path through these swirling spirits. They've had their fill of scattering blue by then, and the heavier colors, the reds and the oranges, they finally get their turn to shine through, painting the sky with the hues of a glorious pirate's treasure.

So there ye have it, me lad! 'Tis the play of the sun's light on the invisible spirits stirred from the sea itself, painting our grand canvas above in a hue fit for any self-respecting buccaneer. Now, if ye'll excuse me, this talk of the sea has made me thirsty for a tankard of grog! Yo ho ho!
